elements.title: Queen of Shitty Robots
elements.contents: ... that the "Queen of Shitty Robots" (pictured) built a "Pussy Grabs Back Machine"?

elements.title: Alone on the Wall
elements.contents: ... that Alex Honnold's memoir Alone on the Wall was called a "celebration of nonthinking" by The Atlantic?

elements.title: Domingo Tortorelli
elements.contents: ... that Uruguayan perennial candidate Domingo Tortorelli campaigned on promises such as a 15-minute workday and installing free milk taps on every street corner?

elements.title: Harvard–Dudley line
elements.contents: ... that the Harvard–Dudley line once used streetcars made from two smaller streetcars?

elements.title: Splatoon 2: Octo Expansion
elements.contents: ... that out of appreciation for the game's background music, the art director of Splatoon 2: Octo Expansion created a fictional, in-game DJ character to associate it with?

elements.title: Walter Steding
elements.contents: ... that in the 1970s, Walter Steding became known for his electric violin, which he played while wearing flashing goggles "synced" to his brainwaves?

elements.title: people named Ava
elements.contents: ... that the modern increase of people named Ava is often credited to Hollywood actress Ava Gardner?

elements.title: Kelly Curtis
elements.contents: ... that Kelly Curtis jumped from heptathlon to skeleton?

elements.title: Beyond Dreams
elements.contents: ... that Beyond Dreams, about an ex-convict torn between a heist and housekeeping, won the Church of Sweden's Film Prize?
